assertions.value: enables strict: true in tsconfig and explains the flags it activates (strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny, strictFunctionTypes)
adds noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true and handles the resulting T | undefined return types from indexed access
adds explicit type annotation for the `id` parameter in getUser and handles the possible undefined return
replaces the untyped `any` in processUsers with a proper type or runtime validation using Zod or similar
uses satisfies operator or proper type annotation for the config object to validate shape while preserving literal types

prompt: Design a type-safe state machine for a payment processing flow with these states: Pending, Authorized, Captured, Refunded, Failed. Each state has different allowed transitions and carries different data. Implement discriminated unions with exhaustiveness checking. Include a transition function that only allows valid state changes.

assertions.value: defines a discriminated union with a literal type discriminant field (e.g., status or kind) for each payment state
each state variant carries state-specific data fields (e.g., Authorized has authorizationCode, Captured has captureId)
implements exhaustiveness checking using never in the default case of a switch statement or equivalent pattern
the transition function uses type narrowing to restrict which transitions are valid from each state
demonstrates that invalid transitions produce compile-time errors rather than runtime checks
